mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market, which typically leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 4.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.2 in a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO and PAYEMS on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term's coefficient drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) lead to higher discount rates and reduced equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns exceeds -0.3 in a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate revenue.
failure_condition: The combined effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term's coefficient drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the CPI inflation rate above 0.5% signals rising inflation pressures, prompting the Fed to tighten monetary policy and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL increases > 0.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns exceeds -0.2 in a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate strong labor market conditions, which typically translate to higher consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances with positive PAYEMS changes, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increased manufacturing activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings and economic growth.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
